file_name: SightLife Surgical, Inc. - STRATEGIC SALES _ MARKETING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: Surgical agrees that it shall disclose promptly to Imprimis all inventions, ideas, concepts, and discoveries, including but not limited to processes, methods, formulas, biological materials, specimens, chemical compounds, formulations, software, data, techniques, products, applications, systems, procedures, technical information, drawings, reports and designs as well as improvements and modifications thereof and know-how thereto (whether or not protectable by copyright, patent, trademark, trade secret or any other proprietary rights), that it makes, conceives of, discovers or develops as a result of the receipt of Confidential Information (as defined in the Confidentiality Agreement referenced in Section 6 below) ("Work Product"). Surgical agrees that all Work Product shall be the sole and exclusive property of Imprimis.
perturbation.changed_text: Surgical agrees that it shall disclose promptly to Imprimis all inventions, ideas, concepts, and discoveries, including but not limited to processes, methods, formulas, biological materials, specimens, chemical compounds, formulations, software, data, techniques, products, applications, systems, procedures, technical information, drawings, reports and designs as well as improvements and modifications thereof and know-how thereto (whether or not protectable by copyright, patent, trademark, trade secret or any other proprietary rights), that it makes, conceives of, discovers or develops as a result of the receipt of Confidential Information (as defined in the Confidentiality Agreement referenced in Section 6 below) ("Work Product"). Surgical retains ownership of all Work Product, but grants Imprimis a perpetual, irrevocable, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the Work Product.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the ownership of 'Work Product'. Initially, it is stated that all Work Product is the 'sole and exclusive property of Imprimis.' However, the modified text states that 'Surgical retains ownership of all Work Product'. This in-text contradiction creates confusion about who actually owns the Work Product, despite granting Imprimis a license. This affects Section 5.1, which states that all Work Product is Surgical's original work, creating ambiguity over ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Surgical represents and warrants that all Work Product is and shall be Surgical's original work (except for material in the public domain or provided by Imprimis) and, to the best of Surgical's knowledge, does not and will not violate or infringe upon the intellectual property right or any other right whatsoever of any person, firm, corporation or other entity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction regarding ownership of the 'Work Product'. The original text states, 'Surgical agrees that all Work Product shall be the sole and exclusive property of Imprimis,' while the changed text says, 'Surgical retains ownership of all Work Product'. This directly contradicts the clause 'Surgical represents and warrants that all Work Product is and shall be Surgical's original work,' because it creates ambiguity over who owns the Work Product, potentially creating unenforceability or a legal issue. This fits the definition of Ambiguity - In Text Contradiction since key terms (ownership of Work Product) is inconsistently defined.

perturbation.original_text: Imprimis has provided marketing materials and product information to Surgical and the Sales Team which they have deemed adequate in order to allow them to understand the Product and perform the Services. Surgical's Chief Executive Officer and Imprimis' Chief Commercial Officer shall initially meet, and continue to meet on a regular basis, to transition, train and manage the Sales Team in order to allow them to integrate into the existing Imprimis commercial sales and marketing plan and adequately perform the Services during the Term.
perturbation.changed_text: Imprimis has provided marketing materials and product information to Surgical and the Sales Team, but Surgical is solely responsible for ensuring the adequacy of these materials. Surgical's Chief Executive Officer and Imprimis' Chief Commercial Officer shall initially meet, and continue to meet on a regular basis, to transition, train and manage the Sales Team in order to allow them to integrate into the existing Imprimis commercial sales and marketing plan and adequately perform the Services during the Term.
perturbation.explanation: This modification shifts responsibility for the 'adequacy' of marketing materials solely onto Surgical. Originally, the text implies Imprimis ensures the materials are adequate, now it's explicitly Surgical's responsibility to 'ensure' adequacy. This in-text contradiction is problematic because Section 8, says 'The Sales Team shall use only such marketing and other materials for the Product in the Territory as are expressly approved in writing in advance by Imprimis'. But since now, the adequacy relies on Surgical, if something goes wrong, it's difficult to evaluate who to blame, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.location: Statement of Work - Imprimis Support, Training & Marketing
perturbation.contradicted_location: Statement of Work - The Sales Team
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Sales Team shall use only such marketing and other materials for the Product in the Territory as are expressly approved in writing in advance by Imprimis, are consistent in all respects with the external marketing of such Product by Imprimis, include all warnings and instructions applicable for the proper use of the Product, comply with all applicable laws and regulations in the Territory, and do not contain any claims regarding a Product or its performance that the Sales Team does not reasonably demonstrate are supported by such Product or its performance.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a contradiction. The original text implies Imprimis ensures adequacy, but the changed text makes Surgical solely responsible for ensuring the adequacy of marketing materials. However, the contradicted text mandates that the Sales Team can only use materials 'expressly approved in writing in advance by Imprimis.' This creates a conflict because Surgical is responsible for adequacy but Imprimis maintains approval control. This introduces ambiguity and potential liability issues if the materials, deemed adequate by Surgical, are later disapproved by Imprimis, potentially leading to a violation of the agreement. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition where key obligations are defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: Either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time for any reason.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time for any reason, provided that the terminating party provides 180 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original termination clause allows either party to terminate the agreement 'at any time for any reason,' which implies immediate termination is permissible. The modified version adds a requirement of '180 days written notice.' This introduces a direct contradiction: the 'any time' clause is now restricted by the notice period. This new change creates conflict with Section 2.2 'Surgical shall immediately cease to provide any further Services under this Agreement and/or any applicable SOW upon receipt of notice from Imprimis that Imprimis is terminating this Agreement and/or such SOW'. Due to the immediate termination clause, Surgical will have to follow the steps for immediate termination and also the 180 days written notice, since the updated clause is also making reference to that.
perturbation.location: Statement of Work - Term & Termination - 2. Termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Surgical shall immediately cease to provide any further Services under this Agreement and/or any applicable SOW upon receipt of notice from Imprimis that Imprimis is terminating this Agreement and/or such SOW. Upon termination of this Agreement and/or such SOW, Surgical shall be entitled to payment for Services completed prior to such termination. Thereafter, Imprimis shall owe Surgical no further amounts or obligations in law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The original clause allows immediate termination, while the modified clause requires 180 days written notice, creating a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which mandates immediate cessation of services. This ambiguity introduces a strong in-text contradiction, particularly since the termination requirements are different. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' because key terms and obligations are defined inconsistently.
